dev.scope: single-issue: fix scripts/checkpoint-prune.sh inverted-semantics bug (QA O3)

tasks_completed.description: Rewrote scripts/checkpoint-prune.sh pruning algorithm to keep the NEWEST RETENTION commits instead of the OLDEST
tasks_completed.changes: Replaced the broken single-line `new_tip=$(git rev-list $ref | sed -n ${RETENTION}p)` + `git update-ref $ref $new_tip` approach (which moved the ref BACKWARDS to the RETENTION-th ancestor, keeping total-RETENTION+1 OLD commits) with a correct history-rewrite via `git commit-tree`: (a) collect the newest RETENTION commits from `git rev-list --topo-order $ref | head -n RETENTION`; (b) reverse them oldest-kept -> newest; (c) for each, re-materialize via `git commit-tree TREE [-p PREV_NEW]` preserving tree SHA, message, author-name/email/date, committer-name/email/date — the first (oldest-kept) becomes parentless; (d) sanity check that the rewritten tip's tree SHA equals the original tip's tree SHA; (e) CAS update-ref from original tip to new tip (aborts cleanly on concurrent writer); (f) run reflog-expire + gc only if at least one ref was rewritten.
tasks_completed.rationale: QA O3 verified live on a 206-commit scratch repo: original script rewrote the ref to 7 commits keeping `base, ck 1..ck 6` and discarded `ck 7..ck 205`. Fix makes the script actually retain `ck 6..ck 205` (200 most recent) on a 206-commit ref with RETENTION=200.

fix_description.root_cause: `git rev-list $ref` outputs tip-first. The previous algorithm picked the RETENTION-th entry in that list (i.e., the RETENTION-th most-recent commit, which is the OLDEST of the commits we want to keep) and moved the ref TO that commit. Because a ref's reachability is defined by its tip plus ancestry via parent pointers, moving the ref backwards shortened the chain to (total - RETENTION + 1) commits, all of them OLDER than the intended keep-set. The safety check `merge-base --is-ancestor` passed because the new tip IS an ancestor of the old tip — it's the SEMANTIC of 'move backwards' that is wrong, not the safety of the move.
fix_description.fix_strategy: Use `git commit-tree` to rebuild the chain. Walk the newest RETENTION commits oldest-kept -> tip, recreating each commit with its original tree/message/author/committer/date metadata but with the new parent chain. The oldest-kept commit becomes a parentless root; each subsequent commit is chained onto the previous rewritten commit. update-ref moves the ref atomically to the new tip via CAS. Old commits become unreachable and are cleaned by reflog-expire + gc.
fix_description.why_not_simpler: Two alternatives were considered and rejected: (a) `git reflog expire --expire=<date> --all && git gc` — doesn't actually shorten the chain because the ref's ancestry via parent pointers still reaches the old commits regardless of reflog. (b) `git replace --graft` — introduces a replacement object, not a real chain trim; inspection tools may or may not honor it. History rewrite via commit-tree is the only approach that produces a truly RETENTION-long chain where the ref's own ancestry contains exactly RETENTION commits.
fix_description.metadata_preservation: Tree SHA, commit message (via `-F msgfile` to preserve newlines/body), author-name, author-email, author-date (ISO-8601 via %aI), committer-name, committer-email, committer-date are all preserved per original commit. Only the parent linkage is rewritten. The tip's tree SHA is verified identical to the original tip's tree SHA before the ref is updated — if they diverge, the rebuild is aborted and the ref is left untouched.
fix_description.concurrency_safety: CAS update-ref uses the snapshotted original tip as the expected old value. If a concurrent writer appends to the ref between our snapshot and the update-ref call, the update-ref fails and the rebuild is reported as failed (FAILED_COUNT incremented, exit 1 if any ref failed).

ac_results.description: Scratch repo with 10 commits on refs/checkpoints/master, RETENTION=5 -> ref has exactly 5 commits, they are the NEWEST 5, tip tree SHA unchanged
ac_results.evidence: Ran prune on 10-commit chain. Output: `refs/checkpoints/master: 10 -> 5 commits (rewrote chain, new tip 7d18cab0aebf)`. After: count=5, tip_msg='ck 9', messages in order = ck 9, ck 8, ck 7, ck 6, ck 5 (newest 5 preserved). Tip tree SHA before == tip tree SHA after (`PASS` on identity check).

ac_results.description: Scratch repo with 3 commits, RETENTION=5 -> no-op (ref untouched)
ac_results.evidence: Output: `refs/checkpoints/master: 3 commits (<= 5) — no pruning needed` / `pruned=0 skipped=1 failed=0`. BEFORE tip == AFTER tip, BEFORE count (3) == AFTER count (3), gc skipped.

ac_results.description: After prune, `git log refs/checkpoints/master --oneline | wc -l` == RETENTION (matched against the QA failure scenario: 206 commits, default RETENTION=200)
ac_results.evidence: Ran prune on 206-commit chain with default RETENTION=200. Output: `refs/checkpoints/master: 206 -> 200 commits (rewrote chain, new tip 9aedbd085669)`. After: count=200, tip_msg='ck 205', oldest-kept_msg='ck 6' (i.e. kept ck 6..ck 205 = 200 newest). Second run reports `200 commits (<= 200) — no pruning needed` (idempotent).

ac_results.description: Verify tree at tip still contains the latest content (no tree corruption)
ac_results.evidence: On 10-commit test: BEFORE_TIP_TREE == AFTER_TIP_TREE (identity check PASS). On 206-commit test: same identity check PASS (the script's internal sanity check at lines ~182-187 compares `git rev-parse ${current_tip}^{tree}` to `git rev-parse ${new_tip}^{tree}` and aborts the rebuild if they differ). `git fsck` on the rewritten repo reports zero errors/missing/broken objects.

blast_radius.notes: grep over /root/.claude confirms the script is only referenced in docs/ (BA spec, QA report, context) and scripts/INDEX.md. No hook, no other script, no command, and no systemd unit invokes it. Risk scope is limited to manual operator invocation.

self_verification.notes: bash -n /root/.claude/scripts/checkpoint-prune.sh: SYNTAX OK. All 4 acceptance criteria executed against scratch repos — all pass. The critical QA failure scenario (206 commits, RETENTION=200) now correctly retains the 200 newest commits.

diff_stats.justification_for_overage: Fix requires replacing a broken 5-line algorithm with a correct chain-rebuild algorithm. Correct algorithm must: (a) collect newest N commits, (b) reverse iteration order, (c) for each commit, extract tree + message + author metadata + committer metadata + both dates, (d) re-materialize each commit via `git commit-tree` with preserved metadata and new parent, (e) sanity-check tree equivalence at tip, (f) CAS update-ref. Each of these steps has minimum-viable representation in bash; the `rebuild_ref` helper function is the smallest reasonable encapsulation. Line count dominated by the seven `git log -1 --format=...` metadata extractions (required to preserve blame/author history) and the two near-identical `git commit-tree` branches (root vs with-parent). No way to reduce meaningfully without sacrificing metadata preservation — which would regress blame integrity on the kept commits.

dev.qa_notes: Run the same scratch-repo test from QA iter1 (206 commits on refs/checkpoints/master, RETENTION=200) — expect count=200, tip message='ck 205', oldest-kept message='ck 6'. Also verify: (a) 3-commit no-op, (b) tip tree SHA unchanged after prune, (c) second run idempotent no-op, (d) `git fsck` clean on rewritten repo. On the LIVE /root/.claude repo itself the ref is currently at 92 commits (< 200 threshold) so invoking the script is a no-op; the algorithm fix is proven in scratch repos only.

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: the prune script was introduced in the iter1 ops-gap work and landed on refs/checkpoints/* via checkpoint mechanism (per context JSON, iter1 landed at dafb290)
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: The iter1 BA spec said 'trim each ref to RETENTION commits' but the implementer translated that as 'move the ref back to the RETENTION-th most-recent commit', which is the OPPOSITE operation. The safety check (merge-base --is-ancestor) masked the bug because moving backwards IS a valid ancestry move — just the wrong direction semantically.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: Replace the single-line sed+update-ref with a full chain rebuild via git commit-tree. The ref's tip remains at the newest content (tree SHA preserved) while the parent chain is truncated to exactly RETENTION commits. This matches the operator's intuition ('I have 206 checkpoints, trim to the 200 newest') and preserves blame metadata on every kept commit.

recommendations: Consider adding a periodic systemd timer (or weekly cron) to invoke scripts/checkpoint-prune.sh automatically; current fix makes such automation safe.
If the production repo ever approaches 200 commits on refs/checkpoints/master (currently 92), the script is now safe to run manually — expect one full gc cycle after first invocation.
Future enhancement: add a --dry-run flag to print the intended rewrite without executing update-ref. Not required by current AC set.
